Our turnover rate is lower than last year.

Murielle, I have the turnover numbers for both regions. Which one looks stronger?
Let me see. The South region is bigger than the North, but its turnover is higher.
Interesting. So the North is more efficient, even though it is smaller.
Exactly. The disparity is significant. The North retains staff far better than the South.
And costs? Is the North more expensive to run?
No, it is roughly the same. The margin in results is wider than the margin in cost.
So our recommendation is clear. The North model is better than the South model.
Yes. I will present it as the more competitive option for the whole company.

Hello team. Here is a quick comparison of our two sites. The head office is larger than the branch, but its turnover is considerably higher. The branch is smaller, yet it performs better on almost every metric. The gap in engagement is significant. The branch is also more cost-effective, because its overtime is lower. The difference in results is much wider than the difference in size. In short, the smaller site is more competitive than the bigger one. I think the branch is the stronger model, and we should learn from it.

Good morning. Let me compare this year with last year. Our turnover is slightly lower, which is good news. The disparity between departments, however, is bigger than before. Sales has a much higher attrition ratio than Finance. The margin between our best and worst teams is wider this year. There is also a clear discrepancy between our targets and our results. Still, overall we are more efficient than last year, and our costs are more comparable across regions. I am confident that next year will be even stronger.
